perturbation.original_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the duration of confidentiality protection from three years to one year creates a direct contradiction with section 9, which states the agreement remains in effect for two years, conflicting with how long Confidential Information must be protected under section 2. This creates uncertainty regarding the actual term of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the confidentiality period from three years to one year. The contradicted text states that the agreement remains in effect for two years and that the requirement to protect Confidential Information survives termination. The change creates a contradiction because the one-year confidentiality period in the changed text is shorter than the two-year agreement duration specified in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty about the actual term of confidentiality, as explained.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by email or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential solely by marking it, in the case of written materials. Oral disclosures or unmarked written materials will not be considered confidential, regardless of notification.
perturbation.explanation: Restricting the method of designating information as confidential to only 'marking' written materials, and explicitly excluding oral or unmarked written notification creates a contradiction with paragraph 5(d), which allows for approved release of information, implying that information can become non-confidential, regardless of initial marking. This change makes it unclear whether oral agreements or subsequent written approvals are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits confidential information designation to marked written materials, explicitly excluding oral or unmarked written notification. This contradicts the original text, which allowed for notification via various means. The contradicted text (d) allows for approved release of information, which now has ambiguity, since the new rule on confidential information conflicts with the ability to approve the release of information that may have originally been shared orally.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party; however, the Recipient shall have a non-exclusive license to use such information for internal business purposes.
perturbation.explanation: While the original text states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the disclosing party, the altered text grants the Recipient a 'non-exclusive license' for internal business use. This creates a direct contradiction as a 'license' implies rights granted to the Recipient, conflicting with the original statement that no rights are conferred. This contradiction is at odds with the restrictions imposed in section 2 regarding disclosure to third parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party ("Recipient") shall, for a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the 'Recipient' a 'non-exclusive license' to use confidential information for internal business purposes, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's restriction on disclosing such information to any third party (including contractors) without prior written approval. The original text explicitly stated that the other party is not granted any rights to the confidential information, therefore a non-exclusive license for the recipient is contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party's Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products similar to the Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient complies with all obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not be construed to limit' to 'shall be construed to limit,' this clause now directly contradicts the intent of the section. The original intent was to allow independent development even with similar information. The altered version makes it unclear whether development of similar products is permitted, despite the acknowledgement of ongoing similar projects, particularly when considering section 5 which discusses information obtained independently. This creates in-text contradiction about the extent of restricted development.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text now states that the agreement 'shall be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products similar to the Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts section (c) of the 'Contradicted Text', which states that information is not deemed confidential if it 'Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder'. The change introduces a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the recipient's ability to independently develop similar products, as the changed text limits this right, while the contradicted text explicitly allows it under certain conditions. This is a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties and in no way creates an obligation for either Party to disclose information to the other Party or to enter into any other agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties and creates an obligation for each Party to disclose all relevant Confidential Information to the other Party to facilitate potential future agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states there is 'no obligation' to disclose information, while the modified text states there 'creates an obligation' to disclose 'all relevant Confidential Information'. This creates a direct contradiction. Furthermore, it conflicts with the clause in section 1 that says 'Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential solely by marking it, in the case of written materials. Oral disclosures or unmarked written materials will not be considered confidential, regardless of notification.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by stating that an obligation to disclose information exists, where previously it did not. This new obligation conflicts with the contradicted text, which allows for optional disclosure of Confidential Information only if it's marked as such. The changed text mandates disclosure, regardless of marking, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect until terminated by either Party giving immediate notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall not survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the agreement duration from a fixed two-year period to 'until terminated by either Party giving immediate notice' directly contradicts the clause that the protection of confidential information 'shall survive termination.' The altered clause explicitly states it will not survive termination. This in-text contradiction creates significant ambiguity regarding the lifespan of the confidentiality obligations after the agreement is terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect until terminated by either Party giving immediate notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall not survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement to be terminable immediately, which directly contradicts the provision stating that confidentiality obligations do not survive termination. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the longevity of confidentiality obligations, as the altered agreement stipulates that they cease upon termination, in the contradicted text
